tickets.question: Do contextual CRT/CRUSH/MOTION views appear in the correct locations without covering controls?
Do LOCK/HEAD/RECEIVER/INTERFERENCE/CRUSH still retain the intended CATHODE character?
Does CATHODE appear in the expected host/plugin browser under THETA Audio Research?
Does CATHODE instantiate without crash or stuck scan?
Does the UI/build display the expected current version or build identity?
Does audio pass/process correctly with no unexpected silence, crackle, hum, or level jump?
Do presets/session reload preserve the current state correctly?
Do the in-scope formats load as expected: VST3 / AU / Standalone?
Does the install/build command replace the previous build cleanly?
Does CATHODE build, install, appear in the expected host/plugin browser, instantiate, validate, and replace older builds cleanly?
tickets.acceptance: Pass only if the current build installs cleanly, appears under THETA Audio Research where applicable, and opens without scan or instantiation failure.

tickets.question: Does CATHODE pass and process audio without unexpected silence, crackle, hum, unsafe level jumps, or host instability?
tickets.acceptance: Fail immediately for silence, runaway level, persistent crackle, stuck output, or repeatable host crash.
tickets.testMaterial: default preset, INIT/START preset, heavy preset, bypass toggles

tickets.question: Does CATHODE still feel like a receiver/CRT degradation instrument with LOCK, HEAD, INTERFERENCE, CRUSH, and visual character working as one identity?
tickets.acceptance: Pass only if the sonic identity remains clear in level-matched bypass comparisons and on at least three source types.
tickets.testMaterial: drums, bass, vocal/dialogue, synth/drone, full mix as relevant

tickets.question: Across subtle, medium, and extreme settings, does CATHODE remain musically useful instead of only working in one narrow range?
tickets.acceptance: Notes must identify best setting range, failure range, and at least one ideal use case.
tickets.testMaterial: subtle setting, medium setting, pushed/extreme setting

tickets.question: Do LOCK, HEAD, RECEIVER, INTERFERENCE, CRUSH, contextual visual bays, and presets behave musically and visually without regression?
tickets.acceptance: Pass only if each major module/control has audible or visible cause-and-effect and no stale UI/audio state.

tickets.question: Do CATHODE module toggles, modes, macros, bypass states, and extreme settings avoid stuck states, clicks, NaN, runaway feedback, or inconsistent behavior?
tickets.acceptance: Fail if any control path can break audio, desync UI, or require plugin reload to recover.
tickets.testMaterial: extreme parameter sweep, rapid toggle/mode changes

tickets.question: Can CATHODE be read, clicked, resized, and understood without fighting the interface?
tickets.acceptance: Pass only if labels are readable, hit areas feel correct, active states are obvious, and the flow is understandable within 30 seconds.
tickets.testMaterial: default scale, common laptop display, resize if supported

tickets.question: Does CATHODE show the expected version/build identity and no stale legacy product naming in user-facing UI or installed artifacts?
tickets.acceptance: Fail if version is wrong/missing or old product identity leaks into visible labels, package names, or host display.
tickets.testMaterial: UI footer/header, plugin browser, installed bundles

tickets.question: Do CATHODE presets and DAW session reload preserve audio state, UI state, selected modes, and bypass states correctly?
tickets.acceptance: Pass only if save/reopen restores both sound and visible state.
tickets.testMaterial: factory presets, user preset if available, DAW save/reopen

tickets.question: Does CATHODE automation and offline render match realtime playback without clicks, zippering, or missing processing?
tickets.acceptance: Fail if common automated parameters click badly or offline bounce differs obviously from realtime.
tickets.testMaterial: automation lanes, realtime playback, offline bounce

tickets.question: Does CATHODE stay responsive with acceptable CPU, stable meters/visuals, and no denormal spikes during silence or heavy use?
tickets.acceptance: Notes should identify default CPU, heavy CPU, silence behavior, and any UI redraw sluggishness.
tickets.testMaterial: host CPU meter, silence, heavy preset, moving UI/meters

tickets.question: Compared with the previous known-good build, did CATHODE keep the important behavior while avoiding new regressions?
tickets.acceptance: Notes must identify what improved, what regressed, and whether the build is safer than the previous baseline.
tickets.testMaterial: previous known-good build notes, current build

tickets.question: What reference audio, screenshots, tester notes, or external examples should be brought back into the CATHODE project chat?
tickets.acceptance: Pass when there is enough evidence to guide the next build without relying on memory.
tickets.testMaterial: screenshots, audio examples, tester notes, return packet

tickets.question: Based on testing, what is the exact next build direction for CATHODE?
tickets.acceptance: Pass only when the next action is clear: bugfix, DSP pass, UI pass, installer pass, or ship/hold.
